items.title: Why Texas’ Population Boom Is Reshaping Local Mobility
items.desc: Rapid growth across Texas is changing how families move, settle, and build community.

items.title: University of North Texas to cut more than 70 programs and minors to trim $45 million deficit
items.desc: Degrees in linguistics and Latin American and women’s and gender studies will be phased out. LGBTQ, Mexican American, Africana and Asian studies are among 25 undergrad minors that will also be cut.

items.title: Texas has taken over issuing permits to store carbon underground. Here’s what to know.
items.desc: The move of authority to the Texas Railroad Commission from the federal government now makes it easier for energy companies to apply for permits to store carbon underground.

items.title: Texas inmate seeks to stop looming execution after codefendant confesses to double murders
items.desc: In his appeal, James Broadnax, who also wants a new trial, included a signed confession by his cousin saying he committed the 2008 Garland murders.

items.title: Texas accepts some Islamic schools into voucher program after lawsuits
items.desc: Federal lawsuits by parents and schools argued Texas officials engaged in religious discrimination by preventing Islamic private schools from accessing state voucher money.

items.title: Skanska scores $165M Texas A&M biology building
items.desc: The Stockholm-based builder will begin construction on the 185,000-square-foot facility next year for the college, a repeat client.

items.title: Urban rail transit construction in the US trails population growth
items.desc: Cities are opting for less costly bus rapid transit projects over subways and light rail.

items.title: Construction prices spiked at ‘staggering’ rate to begin 2026
items.desc: The latest data — prior to the war in Iran — shows prices rose on an annualized basis of 12.6% through February.

items.title: Fed holds rates steady, flags uncertainty as oil price soars amid war
items.desc: The Fed faces a policy dilemma as the Iran war spurs a jump in energy prices, threatening to increase inflation, slow economic growth and push up unemployment.

items.title: 4 bonus depreciation tax tips for construction firms
items.desc: With IRS staff under pressure, developing internal processes and documenting fixed assets can avoid extended reviews and additional information requests, writes a construction accountant.

items.title: ‘The Pout-Pout Fish’: A Hypnotic Story of Opposites Finding Connection
items.desc: Director Ricard Cussó and writer Elise Allen discuss the challenges and joys of adapting Deborah Diesen’s popular picture book into a mesmerizing animated undersea adventure about an unlikely aquatic duo on a daunting quest to save their homes, hitti

items.title: ‘Yugen’ Mixes Mexican Ceramics with Japanese Philosophy
items.desc: Director Nayelli Ojeda discusses her unique approach to stop-motion filmmaking that blends the fundamental idea behind much of Japan’s art with Mexican culture and Oaxacan clay pottery techniques used on puppets and replacement parts; her directorial

items.title: Blink Industries, Adult Swim SMALLS Debut 'Wilem' 2D Short
items.desc: Directed by James Papper and Bárbara Oliveira, the animated film centers on a Gen-Z barbarian prince who avoids battle in favor of gaming until his parents force him to face his destiny on his 16th birthday.

items.title: Final Frontier Animates Short Film Tribute to 'The Maxx'
items.desc: Directed by Tim Fox, the indie film ‘The Maxx – I’m Out of Control Again!’ revisits Sam Kieth’s seminal 1993 comic and 1995 MTV animated adaptation, exploring the property’s psychological themes through a contemporary visual approach.

items.title: ‘Aces of Justice’ Completes First Season on YouTube
items.desc: The independently produced anime-influenced action series concludes its six-episode debut season while building a growing online audience.
